​@John_Ripper​ 1) in the initial encounter that is on video where service is refused, I didn't see race mentioned. Only mention of politics and a Trump hoodie. Race wasn't mentioned until subsequent explanatory videos. In one she is explaining her side and says "So i ask torta" and another video she's calling for the woman's video to be taken down offline and says "Idk, I think she's Hispanic" and then mentions how white people are being racist in the comments. My point was that these two black girls will get to argue that they didn't feel safe because a white man and his wife came in and he had a Trump sweatshirt and that will be it, it will be argued as not discrimination because it's based on politics. A better comparison would be if a conservative waitress refused to serve a customer because they wore a planned parenthood sweatshirt, or a Trump-supporting waiter refused to serve somebody wearing a Kamala hoodie. But of course, there are no such stories. 

It's really not similar to someone refusing to violate their religious beliefs by catering a gay wedding or filling a Plan B prescription, and it's very telling that those sorts of things are the closest we can get to finding an equivalent to this type of behavior on the right.
